_comment: Public agent plugins, managed declaratively. Apply with: scripts/sync-agent-scripts.sh --provider plugins. NOTHING is vendored here — only the enable-config. CLAUDE is fully declarative: the 'claude' section merges into ~/.claude/settings.json (extraKnownMarketplaces + enabledPlugins); Claude Code resolves/installs the marketplace on next launch. CODEX is mostly declarative: marketplaces are registered via the `codex plugin marketplace add` CLI (the docs say use it rather than hand-editing config.toml), and 'enabled' plugins are written declaratively into ~/.codex/config.toml as [plugins."name@marketplace"] enabled=true — no /plugins TUI needed. 'installCommands' run extra per-plugin steps (e.g. CE's bunx installer for custom agents Codex can't register yet). Per-profile (per-project) plugins live in profiles/<name>/plugins.json with a 'claude' section, merged into each assigned project's .claude/settings.json (Codex plugins are inherently global).

manualSteps: If skills don't show up after sync, run /plugins in Codex once to confirm 'compound-engineering' is installed under the Compound Engineering marketplace, then restart Codex.
